violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food * (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Containers of spaghetti noodles and pasta sauce in the walk in cooler were not dated.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food * was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. A container of blooming sauce in the walk in cooler was dated 12/12/25. This exceeds the ten day limit before it must be discarded.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. The laminate on the edges of the counter under the steamer is coming off and exposing bare wood. The handle and gasket on the chest freezer at the bottom of the stairs are broken, with exposed insulation on the handle. Gaskets on the prep cooler across from the white freezer are broken. Duct tape was placed on the door of the ice cream freezer in the bar.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The can opener and shelves in the white meat cooler need to be cleaned.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The fan on top of the bread rack by the walk in cooler needs to be cleaned.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The ceilings around the vents in the kitchen and floors under the cooking equipment need to be cleaned. Floors in the storage area, dish area, and prep area under the equipment and shelves need to be cleaned.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. Cracked and broken floor tile in the dish area and by the ice machine. Torn and missing flooring in the upstairs kitchen and dish areas.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employee coat was hung on the bread rack over loaves of bread.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
